title: Are Wonton-wrappers Gluten Free? | BiteRight
description: Public gluten safety analysis for Wonton-wrappers. See major risks, safer alternatives, and what to ask before ordering.
heading: Are Wonton-wrappers gluten free?
intro: This public analysis report explains the biggest gluten risks in Wonton-wrappers and how to order more safely.

verdict.summary: Traditional wonton wrappers are typically made from wheat flour, which contains gluten, posing a significant risk for individuals with coeliac disease. Cross-contamination during preparation can further increase gluten exposure.

disclaimer: This guidance is informational only. Always verify ingredients and preparation with the restaurant.

sections.title: Quick answer
sections.body: Wonton-wrappers can vary by recipe, ingredients, and cross-contact controls in the kitchen.

sections.title: Common gluten risks
sections.body: Watch for hidden sources like soy sauce, malt flavoring, marinades, thickeners, and shared fryers.

sections.title: How BiteRight helps
sections.body: Scan menus and ingredient labels with BiteRight to get a localized gluten-risk breakdown in seconds.

waiter_script.preview: Are your wonton wrappers made with wheat flour or are there gluten-free options available?

faq.question: Can BiteRight confirm if Wonton-wrappers are gluten free?
faq.answer: BiteRight highlights likely gluten risks based on ingredients and preparation. Always confirm with the kitchen if you have coeliac disease.

faq.question: What should I ask a restaurant?
faq.answer: Ask about shared equipment, sauces, marinades, and whether the kitchen has a dedicated gluten-free prep area.
